name: libencode-perl
title: libencode-perl
summary: module providing interfaces between Perl's strings and the system
description:
module providing interfaces between Perl's strings and the system
The Encode module provides the interfaces between Perl's strings and the
rest of the system.
.
When Perl is processing "binary data", the programmer wants Perl to process
"sequences of bytes". This is not a problem for Perl - as a byte has 256
possible values, it easily fits in Perl's much larger "logical character".
.
Encodes a string from Perl's internal form into ENCODING and returns a
sequence of octets.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libencode-perl because module providing interfaces between Perl's strings and the system.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libencode-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libencode-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libencode-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
